message:
perf: Wizer as default + pre-built VFS directory index

- build-wizer.sh now outputs directly to python.wasm (single code path)
- build-phase2.sh chains into build-wizer.sh when wizer is available
- Pre-build directory index from 242 stdlib paths at module load, clone
  per request instead of rebuilding from scratch
- Use deletedFiles Set instead of mutating the files object, keeping
  the base stdlib layer immutable across requests
- Pass pre-built index to both worker.ts and python-do.ts

34/34 tests pass.

files.patch:
@@ -363,3 +363,13 @@ info "  Binary: $BUILD_DIR/python.sh"
 echo ""
 info "Test with:"
 info "  $BUILD_DIR/python.sh -c \"print('hello from zig-compiled WASI Python')\""
+
+# Step 9: Wizer pre-initialization (if wizer is available)
+if command -v wizer >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v wasm-opt >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    info ""
+    info "Wizer detected — creating pre-initialized snapshot..."
+    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/build-wizer.sh"
+else
+    info ""
+    info "Tip: install wizer (cargo install wizer --all-features) for ~5x faster cold starts"
+fi

@@ -146,12 +146,12 @@ fi
 # Cleanup intermediate
 rm -f "$WIZER_RAW"
 
-# Copy to worker/src
-cp "$OUTPUT" "$ROOT_DIR/worker/src/python-wizer.wasm"
+# Replace python.wasm — the wizer binary IS the default now.
+# pymode_wizer.c main() falls back to Py_BytesMain if not pre-initialized.
+cp "$OUTPUT" "$ROOT_DIR/worker/src/python.wasm"
 
 echo ""
-echo "Done! python-wizer.wasm"
+echo "Done! python.wasm (wizer snapshot)"
 echo "  Size: $(wc -c < "$OUTPUT" | tr -d ' ' | awk '{printf "%.1fMB", $1/1048576}')"
-echo "  Location: worker/src/python-wizer.wasm"
 echo ""
 echo "Cold start: ~5ms (vs ~28ms without snapshot)"
